reviews.review_text:
I have been playing Evrima for around 18 hours as of posting this so far, while I may not have as much experience as someone with something like 100+ hours. I feel as if I have a opinion on all the apparent controversy surrounding it.
The isle is a hard game, obviously. Though, I think that is what makes it so special. I've seen people take either side, you either love it, or you hate it. I, however, am riding the fence between the two, leaning more toward the side of love. Here area few things I love about the isle, and things I am more discouraged by, or my simplified pros and cons list.

PROS: The isle is very immersive, an amazing and inherently attractive game. Beautiful graphics, realistic mechanisms, and the game has genuinely gained the right to be considered living up to what it was initially hoping to achieve. To avoid the stereotype of being, and I quote; "just another dinosaur game". The isle utterly deserves the credit. According to what I have experienced while playing, it is worth the time and energy you have to dedicate if you really wish to. So yes, I think that if you like the idea of realistic survival, intense gameplay, hardship, have the ever elusive ability to start again after failing, and restrain from rage quitting, get the isle. I promise you won't regret it.

CONS (?): As much as I love the isle, it would be irresponsible of me not to address the cons of the isle. The game requires a few, if not several hours of your time. Your dino will get stronger, and harder to kill as it ages, but that doesn't distract from the fact if you die, you can't regain any of the lost growth or hardship you went through to get yourself there. So the intense realism has a cost, a heavy one. I always see people hating on the game because of how often they have died, or how hard it is to meet the requirements to grow properly. And honestly? I think you should expect it. People who complain and whine about dying over, and over again don't have what it takes to truly appreciate the game as a whole.

MY TAKE: So what do I think? I truly think that the isle is something special, a hidden gem in a pile of gold, especially for only being in beta development so far. But you need dedication and passion for the game. Rinse and repeat. That is all I recommend. If you think your up for the challenge, go for it. Just remember, it's not easy, you will fail, and it will likely take the hard way for you to learn properly. My advice? Do some research, learn a few handy tricks about how to develop your survival skills, ask questions, you'll do great.

That's all.

Died to a cheater, went to their discord to warn people then got banned by Superlunary. The Dev's and discord mods don't care about the cheaters in the game and punish regular players. 

Don't waste your money on this game, it really isn't worth it.
Bought it. Played 1,5 hours. Did not like it. Refunded it. Trash talked it for 2 days. Bought it again the 3rd day. Now I am hooked. GG

I really wanted to like this game. But I don't.

I can accept that it is a hardcore survival game. I know it is not supposed to be easy. But the game has so many issues. It is quite hard to ignore just how rough this game is. The game has been in development, for over 11 years. It doesn't look it. 

The UI is one of the worst I have ever encountered. The title screen looks like something straight out of the 80's. Don't worry about in game tooltips, there aren't any. In fact you don't need to worry about there being any useful information in game at all.

When you do go out to the web to try to find some answers you will struggle. All of the information you will find is old and inaccurate. You will need to dig deep to find any real answers here. There is no map in the game. There are two I have found online, but neither of them are very accurate. 

There are many bugs. 

I have crashed. I have gotten stuck repeatedly. I have spawned in, and been stuck. I have been unable to attack, because I was glitched. I have been unable to bite people, because they were glitched. I have watched my food fall through the world and disappear. I saw flying plants. I saw flying rocks. I saw flying fish. I have found edible plants inside solid rocks. I have watched players and AI alike who who have forgotten how to be animated. There are so many bugs that I could literally bury you in a solid block of 8000 characters just talking about bugs. But there are other things I need to talk about. 

I need to talk about pinning. 

I suggest googling the subject. A quick search, will introduce you to the word "pinslop". Pinning in the Isle is basically a stun. But unlike every other game on the planet pinning in the Isle does not end in just a few seconds. The pin will continue until your attacker chooses to end it, or runs out of stamina. Pinning in the isle will often last until you die.

The true alpha predator of the isle right now, is the allo who can pin anything that is under 70% of it's own weight. All an allo needs to do is land a single right click pounce attack and you are now in "animation jail" as the community has chosen to call it. You cannot move. You cannot fight. You are not playing a game anymore. You are a spectator watching your own death. 

An allo is faster than most prey. Landing your pin is quite easy. Very few things on the Isle are large enough to avoid being pinned. Even fewer are fast enough to run. And even those that are large enough to resist the easy kill by pinning still need to worry. The allo can bring friends. If their combined weight is enough they can once again pin you. And the allo can still pounce which is a potent bleed attack.

Bleeding prey cannot easily escape from an allo. Tracking by a blood trail is quite easy. Tracking without one is also possible, but harder. If an allo can follow you and harass you, there is a very real chance that you will bleed out and die. 

I have played a good variety of things. I like many of the smaller faster growing dinosaurs. They can be fun. And if things go wrong, it does not take long to try again. But allos are everywhere. And pinning, is an ugly way to die. If you have enough friends a large enough gang of smaller dinos can be fun. A friend can attack and help break a pin. But allos often have friends too. 

It is unfortunate because many of the fights that the allo can win by default with their unbeatable gimmick of pinning would be good fights if the allo simply did not have pin. There would be battles again. There would be a game again. As is you have only two real choices. Bring enough friends to be a bigger threat than a few allos, or play something too large to easily pin.

There are only a few dinosaurs that grow large enough to not worry about pinning. The Rex is one of those few, and surprisingly it is in a reasonable place right now.

There is the issue of rex crush however, which is a larger and even more dangerous pin.

The big herbivores have it easy. The Triceratops, and the Stegosaurus can find food easily. You can reasonably expect to have perfect diets for your entire life. This will help you grow quickly. The only real challenge will be predators. You will never be fast enough to run. If anything finds you, be prepared to fight.

Fights as any of these apex dinosaurs can be quite fun. The balance between the rex, and the allo, and the big herbivores is okay. Fights can easily go either way depending on position, numbers, size, skill, and a little bit of luck. I have played as each of them, and I have done well. 

The poor abused deino ( Croc ) in theory at least is also an apex predator. It can potentially grow quite large. It can lunge, and grab, and kill most anything in the game, if it can grow.

Unlike the rex, who has a solid hour to secure it's first meal, a baby deino has less than 20 minutes. Trapped in the water, you will have few opportunities. A baby deino is born too small to hunt anything but the smallest of prey.

You will probably hear some chickens. The challenge will be finding one in the tall grass, and getting close enough to bite it. One bite will kill it, but chickens will run if you get close. Unless you are lucky enough to find a chicken that is stuck, you are more likely to watch it run away than to catch a meal. 

If you do manage to find food, you will want to wait. None of the food that is realistically available to a baby deino is on your diet. So you will want to spend 8 of the precious few minutes that are available to you, trying to rot food to get diets. Maybe you will even find a dead deer or boar that someone has helpfully left dead near the water. But they will take over 20 minutes to rot. You can tear chunks off, which will rot more quickly, but you will still want to wait. 

While you wait, everyone else in the area, can smell your food. Other bigger hungrier dienos will eagerly gobble you up and steal your food just to buy themselves a few more precious seconds to survive. 

I do realize that deino, is an apex predator. I do realize that it cannot be too easy. I do realize that if life was too easy for the deino, that they would be just as much of a threat as the allo currently is. 

But there are better ways this could be handled. Making the deino so small and hungry and desperate is a very strange choice. A rex is just as dangerous and has a much easier life. The allo is even easier, grows much faster, and it can pin.

I don't understand this developer at all. He make decisions that are truly baffling to me. I never would have even imagined ever seeing a game with such a long stun ( pin ) effect. I never would have imagined that the patient merciless hunter of the water would starve to death faster than some of the smallest creatures in the Isle. And yet here we are.

The developer plans to add humans to the game. Nobody wants this. But it is still going to happen. Because that's just how this developer rolls. 

To be fair the servers are still full. People do still play this game. I even still play this game on occasion. Somehow despite it's many flaws the game does still manage to have that undefinable something that keeps bringing people back. But with the state it is in I can not in good conscious give it a positive review. And with this developer, I don't see that as something that is likely to ever change.

The Isle does have dinosaurs. The Isle is large. The Isle is pretty. But it has so many problems. It has so many bugs. Development is painfully slow, and the direction it is taking is truly baffling. 

The Isle may look pretty from a distance. But it is not. It is ugly. It is cruel. It is not your friend.

I have had a few sessions on this game so far with some friends and I am personally having a good time. Obviously the proximity chat you see in videos online is a mod and has its own dedicated community and build of the game needed to run it (which you can change the base game to at anytime when you opt into it through steam) and I would like to warn people that if you want the proximity chat like in the reels and tiktoks? you need the mod for it since its not in the base game. However the base game is still  pretty cool. Tons of servers (lots of which are Chinese servers with 9,999 ping) and the community from my experience is pretty chill. If you are a dino lover like I am, check this game out. its really cool to survive and grow old as a dinosaur and try out different types of dinos BUT growing does take a bit of time so if you die? you have to restart as a baby. That can be annoying when starting out and learning the controls but there are servers dedicated to new players which kind of helps. Additionally, (and this is my only real complaint) depending on the server you join, not all dinosaurs may be enabled at the start when you pick a dinosaur to start out as and are locked behind a servers dedicated discord server to unlock. some servers lock dinosaurs like the Spinosaurus behind their discord which IS still free if you don't mind joining their discord just to get access to it as well as other dinosaurs but can be a huge turn off for some players. Despite that, I would recommend to give this game a shot especially when its on sale.

Hello guy or girl reading this review.

I played this game for about 80 hours, and I have enjoyed some of my time in the game. However, when I started getting good at the game and survived for a long time, I found that my dinos got deleted.

The reason that I will no longer be playing this game and will not recommend it is that I just lost another T-Rex because the servers reset, and then when I logged back on, the dino was gone. I was on an official server, EU2.

This has happened before, but this time it was a Rex that I spent about 8–10 hours on, which was just about to reach Prime.

If the devs see this and fix it, then I will take down the review. But it is UNACCEPTABLE that I can lose about 10 hours of progress due to bad game management.

If you like dinosaurs then this game is good, however there is much room for improvement. Quality of life updates would be great, there are many that could be done. The game can at times be pretty frustrating though, its a time investment and ultimately the time you invest to grow can be cut short pretty unfairly. If you can look past that though, its an enjoyable experience on the right servers.
it is super realistic and fun great to play with friends

it's a dino eat dino world out there.

edit: if you bait friendliness but then eat me while I'm trying to get to prime alpha trex same as you, I hope you stub your toe on everything in your home. Other than that, the game is fun.

edit: Deinos when I'm a Deino especially too. ♥♥♥♥ you guys.

The Isle is not a game. The Isle is a double handful of character designs dropped into a decent-looking map with the world's laziest survival mechanic to tie it all together. By the time you finish growing your dinosaur to adult, after dying a thousand times to other players for the crime of existing and being seen by them, you'll be so sick of the survival portion of the game, so sick of the lack of other interesting things to do, that you'll resort to doing what everyone else does - fighting every other player you see. The only problem with this plan is that the Isle is about as effective of a "dinosaur fighting game" as it is a "dinosaur survival game", which is to say it is an exceptionally poor one. Your dino will have two moves it can make, those moves will be 90's-era hitbox based, and oh yeah, for some random reason some dinos get to have one of their moves be a pinslop move that holds their opponent down until said opponent dies. YAY DINOSAUR FIGHTING. 

Inevitably, likely very quickly, this whole-lotta-nothing will get old for you and you'll fall back on the final refuge of the Isle fandom - forming a mega/mixpack. "There's safety in numbers" will be your justification, but within minutes of joining said group, you'll be cruising around the map and murdering every solo you encounter with your newfound group. You'll think to yourself "those loser solos just need to GIT GOOD", all the while ignoring that the only reason you're in a group is that you never GOT GOOD, there is no GETTING GOOD, because there is not a game here to GIT GOOD at. Just a bunch of bored suckers trying their best to strip any shine left off of this turd. 

Don't buy The Isle. Don't buy it's competitor. Expect more from game developers. Demand they actually release a real game for you to play, not just allow you access to their bootstrapped dev sandbox. Accept nothing less.

Unfortunately we had to request a refund for this after doing research me and my wife decided to purchase this.

The game was enticing and looked fun and a cool concept, but there was some problems.

First initial install we were met where my wife could only see chinese servers and I couldnt see any, come to find out we need to switch to a beta version of the game and let it re-install (8gb no biggie).

Upon switching to the beta (envima) version the game could no longer find the anti-cheat file it had previously installed.

Again after some troubleshooting and research we found out that we needed to run the batch file for it to install.

Got it installed yay! No taco just yet tho..We tried to join up on some servers but all were either full or we didnt have the right game version. So after more time troubleshooting and staring at the server listing we decided to refund.

If your solo go for it, but if your with a group, god speed

Man I used to love this game and came back to it a lot over the years but I just can not recommend it in its current state. AI spawns are broken, the new map is awful and boring, and I can not stand the new Elder system. Who wants to spend 12 hours growing a Dino to full age just for it to end up weaker than younger dinos? 

The Elder system especially sucks if you choose to play Croc or any of the large/medium dinos. You are weak and useless for hours and hours as you grow. You finally hit 85% and no other crocs can grab you out of the water. You get maybe 2 hours of being strong, then you are weak and useless again. Boring. It feels like there is zero payoff for the time you spent surviving and it didn't use to be like that. The 
end game was essentially removed with  the elder system. Entombing is a cool new feature but I will not play this game with the Elder system.

There are many other issues with the game and they all have to do with new systems/mechanics. It really boils down to this game moving in the wrong direction over the past year and a half or so.

EDIT: For anyone seeing this, unofficial server Dino Den has tools on their website that solve some of these problems. I am leaving the review as is because the base game and most community servers still have the issues but some community servers make it easier to experience the good part of the game.

This game hates the player. There is a huge difference between "hardcore survival" and "bad game design".
Everyone says the official servers are too toxic (already a huge red flag!!), so I played the best unofficial servers. They turn off AI food spawns so you are forced to go to the sanctuary to find food as a baby dinosaur.

- the swamp sanctuary you have to swim to, which takes 15 minutes, and never has any players in it. Whenever I spawn and see I am near that I want to kill my dino immediately. 
- the highlands sanctuary always seems to have 30 players in it and turns into a massive battle royale. This is by far the best one to spawn near and you get murdered 70% of the time.
- the jungle sanctuary is camped by Troodon 100% of the time, a dinosaur that has special privileges to go after babies. 10 of them sit on a rock, murder a baby when it comes by, and sit on the rock again. It is immensely boring to play - I've done it - and makes the early game hell for everyone else. Why is this thing in the game?

There is no reason to ever play herbivore, they have nothing to do except die to big carnivores and they are currently in the process of nerfing the best herbivores into the ground on the beta branch (hordetest).

There is a good game in here somewhere - when you finally manage to grow an adult dinosaur and group up with your own kind for battles for survival, it's AWESOME, but you spend 5% of your time doing that and 95% either sitting in a bush (literally) or being a useless early game dino dying to troodon and other spawn campers.

Because of these issues, there is no way to learn how to play the game. I want to learn adult dino matchups and you never actually get to grow up to fight adult dinos to learn them. You get murdered by players who have been playing for years longer than you before you ever get to take your adult for a fight. 

Do not waste your time with this game. Hopefully someone comes along one day and makes another of the same genre that actually cares about players having fun and doesn't mask every huge game design issue as "it's a survival game it's supposed to be hard". Do better, the Isle.

I really want to give this a good rating, because the concept is SO GOOD. The only problem is the devs truly don't give a ♥♥♥♥. I have probably died more times to random BS like crashing or glitches then I have to the environment or other players. In the 170 hours I've played thus far I crashed around 13, had to log because of a bug around 10 and encountered 2 hackers. I've probably come across 30 different bugs, and a lot of them you'll experience multiple times in a single run. The devs only do major bug fixes in the test servers, and then actually put them in the main game 3 months later. Yet, even with all of this, I still keep coming back because there's really nothing else to go to. The only game that can even come close right now is Path Of Titans, but Path Of Titans scratches a completely different itch. This year is supposed to be their best year and they have been making some progress, but the fact still stands that this game is in a ♥♥♥♥ hole. I really really hope that they can get their ♥♥♥♥ together and make this a game that I don't have to be ashamed of playing.

this is exactly the game i was looking for, ive played dayz, minecraft but neither got the survival feeling as good as the isle. For me it all came down to picking a strategy, playstyle and adapting it by choosing the right dinosaur. Also the variety of dinosaurs allows for very different playstyles so its hard to get bored. The game seems easy to learn since the combat is not just skill but mostly size, so its not hard  for someone with 40 hours. even at the lowest graphics the game looks beutiful and i can play on 60fps on a mid pc. Very good game

We really didn't have fun with this as a duo. It was annoyingly boring,  waste of time, and overall a bad, frusturatung experience as a player. The game does spell it out that this is a realism based survival game, but honestly other games base themselves the same way and are able to atleast make fun mechanics making the GAME worthwhile to play. 
In this game if you decide to join, you don't have things to do, you don't have any driving factor nor do you have any direction. You just have to eat, wait over an hour to grow (basically meaning you have to go AFK for over an hour just to get any progress), and then you'll end up dying to something pretty quickly. 

This gameplay loop is just boring. You are able to join a family, but in most cases you'll die before you're able to fully grow/experience being a somewhat grown dinosaur. This, paired with the amount of time it takes and lack of things to do in the game make it an incredible bore to behold and I wouldn't recommend it to anyone. Most of my 9 hours were spent debugging, getting passed a very poor Ui, and sitting in a single place to eat before moving on after an hour to eat some more. There is no substance here and I would have to recommend looking elsewhere for an enjoyable dinosaur experience. Also the Devs just are not updating the game and sales are probably relying on these VOIP servers that have gotten popular on shorts/reels (this is not a native feature it is a mod).  Highly do NOT recommend

Multiple times on official servers I safely log out and the next day my dinosaur is just gone. It shows the dino in the server screen, but when i go to log in it disappears. Their databases must be trash. Wasted enough time not dying in the game to get wiped by their servers..

I definitely would not recommend this game.

At first, the game feels enjoyable and rewarding. You can easily spend 35 hours growing a Rex to adulthood, expecting to finally enjoy the endgame experience. However, the major issue is the constant bugs and server problems. Even if you log out safely, there is a chance your dinosaur can randomly disappear or die due to glitches.

Coming back the next day only to find your fully grown dinosaur deleted is extremely frustrating, especially after investing dozens of hours into progression. At that point, you are forced to start over from scratch and repeat the same grind again.

The game has potential and can be fun initially, but the technical issues completely ruin the long-term experience. Instead of enjoying your progress, you end up worrying about losing everything because of bugs. In my opinion, it is not worth the money in its current state. Save your $20 and spend it on something more stable and polished.
game has the worst community ive ever been apart of. dont ever download

At first, it may seem so perfect to finally have a realism survival dino game... then you pour hours and hours and hours into the game only to relog back in and have all progress wiped. admins take no accountability for server crashes/bugs that have been happening for YEARS and when voicing these issues through the proper channels on discord, they chat ban you. It's actually a damn shame.
This game is simply a scam: a bug that keeps reoccurring deletes all your progress. Can't even get the most simple key feature right.
As of latest update, I spawned in a a juvenile Allosaurus. Saw a boar and bit it 57 times (believe me, I counted). The boar got away because I ran out of stamina. Don't buy this game.

This game would be amazing, but Dondi and team keep screwing everyone over with the bugs and lack of support. Your discord admins are an absolute joke, replace them. I'm going to keep playing the game though because I'm an idiot.

Issues with game

3 main types of servers

Roleplay servers- There are only 2 of these servers you are most likely seen videos of these servers issue is with there only being 2 they are always full unless you a jobless loser who spends hours waiting to get in or for a restart you will never get to play this server. These are also the only servers with VOIP... IF YOU DO GET IN YOU WILL HAVE A FUN TIME ITS THE ONLY FUN YOU WILL HAVE IN THE GAME

Community servers- 70% are PTW always trying to sell grown dinos or in game perks so they suck and the other 30% are either dead or have crazy high grow rates so they are just Deathmatch's

Official servers- dont even think about it  they only have 115 people on are full of people mix packing and you will only ever see people around the river delta the whole rest the map is empty and cheaters run around everywhere


I wouldnt recommend the game it is 11 years old and still in Early Access the game is never updated or fixed and has all been ditched by the developers and is only kept alive by a small amount of community servers

spend hours growing a dinosaur in a giant world with no map, providing zero functional AI to actually feed yourself. The fish AI is completely broken, land AI is nonexistent, and if you make one mistake or starve, you lose all your progress and start back at zero. It’s not "hardcore survival," it’s just poorly optimized, empty, and boring. waiting on a refund fs

i loved this game, it is kind of hard at first but u you'll get better. I loved it, i made some friends along the way. But i would recommend to like watch videos first before buying a game u don't know if u are gonna play it.

Its crazy how a game released back in 2015 can be this unfinished. Very little variation of dinos and colors to choose from. Spent around 10 hours of my time growing a dinosaur just for it to vanish the next day when I log in. Game is unfinished and honestly not worth playing at this current state. I aint spending half a day to grow a dinosaur just for it to either dissapear or get killed by another dino whos either cheating or dying because of the games insanely bad optimazation. The other dino could be at close range in my screen when in reality hes further away. Fix this fkn game already. Deleted and maybe come back to it 2050 maybe they will have fixed some of these issues

Player base/community is absolutly insufferable. Constantly ruining the game for others. Killing for fun, killing for ego. I'm sick of dying (to my own kind) over and over again or due pathetic and small-d-energy players who can't stand to see other players (trying) to have fun or grow their dino to a potentionally better and bigger dino than their own.
HOWEVER.. The scenery in this game is so beautiful, details in the dinos as well. Like their skin, each dino having different animations in eating, drinking, etc. 
It's so cute to watch your baby dino grow and how their movement and noises change over time. 
Beautiful game but with the worst player base I have encountered yet.
Play for hours to grow a dino then server crashes and all progress lost forced to start again ffs
not much to do but eat and drink.  fighting is limited.  this game is more of a social setting than a game itself.  also, 6 hours of gametime to grow to adult, but one death and youre back to infant

this game is beyond ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ stupid love everthing about the game but the fact that devs dont do ♥♥♥♥ for the cheaters is insane i spend litteral hours playing on one character to then get one shot by the weakest thing in the game which has happened countless times if you want to waste hours of your time go for it!
Dope game, you gotta be thick skinned though and ready to lose hours of progress.

this game is amazing, wish i found it before, the animation are cool and the growing part is the best part, it's so satisfying to see your dino growing up even if some dinos are really slow like the t-rex.
There's only one problem, when someone grabs you, you basically are done for, there's have to be something to break free but still the game is really good and grabbing other dinos is a little hard so i'm fine with it.

My very second dinosaur ever in the game, which I played for 4 hours, a T-rex, is gone, I for sure logged it out correctly, and when I came back a day later (Today) to continue playing, I saw my dino on the menu listing, but when I joined the server, I was at the dinosaur selection screen. 

If this happened on my FIRST TWO DAYS.. ON MY SECOND DINOSAUR.... the probability of it happening again is huge. I'm sorry but DO NOT waste your money on this.

It was an Official server (Official SA 1) and the game has not been update since I bought it on the 1st of July, the last update was the 30th May.
It is utterly bewildering why this game has such good reviews. Constantly crashes, and couldn't find one other player in an hour of playing on a fully populated server.

The game offers an engaging gameplay experience, featuring a diverse variety of dinosaurs and impressive graphics, provided your PC meets the system requirements. Like many in the community, I am looking forward to future updates introducing new dinosaur species and, hopefully, the implementation of a proximity voice chat feature in the near future.
the best bad game i've ever played (bad in terms of bugs and optimization issues) - even with the bugs its amazing to play if you have some tolerance for game crashes
if you want to fully enjoy this game, get the bosch island server website and follow the guide to get proximity chat. the game does not have any proximity chat

Fun in concept, but failed execution. Imagine agar.io, but the larger player is also faster than the smaller. Meaning this game has fundamental game design issues that the dev refuses to fix, on top of the other bugs in the game. 

What players do: pick top meta dino like rex or croc, hide in the far corner of the map avoiding interaction for literal days at a time while growing. If they spent enough time afking and combat logging successfully, they are now untouchable by 95% of the server.

The dev says this is okay because it's a survival game, not a pvp game. But if you look at this as a survival game, it's even worse. The PVE spawns break entirely, fish swim in the air, ai gets stuck in terrain, and their basic avoidance after seeing you through trees or rocks is simpler than the ghosts in pacman.

That's not even touching on the cheaters, bugged mechanics, and bad server performance (you will rubber band on official servers even with perfect ping).

I gave it more time than I should have, thinking maybe a different dino would change the experience. It doesn't. It's the same as the Queens problem: the different dinos mean you get the choice to play as a pawn or play as a queen. But worse, because in this game pawns can't take Queens.

The idea is so GOOD. The execution is so POOR.  

The game defaults to "legacy" which is no longer supported, so unless you dig through the info yourself after purchasing the game you wont be able to find official servers. It does not by default install the anti cheat on some systems at the same time, making you go into the files and activate it manually.

The netcode is some of the worst I've ever seen, and I played Ark:SE for thousands of hours. Every encounter is a coin flip of did you lag or did they.  Weird rules around AI spawning force everyone to be in popular areas, making the maps a lot smaller than they appear, several of them do not spawn at all. Unless you are playing on an unofficial server you cannot communicate with dinosaurs not of your direct species, essentially removing most social aspect from the game.  

Despite all this i cant keep myself from playing, hoping the game runs properly when its time to actually do combat and being let down time and time again, official server, modded, unmodded it all just runs like trash.

this game is in such a bad state even after a decade of work. if its not cheaters using nametag hacks its glitches and downright broken mechanics that will get you. as someone with thousands of hours all i can say is the dev team just DOES NOT CARE.

First of all, amazing game, love it. 
But I don't get why you need the anti-cheat if there are hackers anyway.
EU 7 official server, 73% grown Trick, 9T weight, insta killed by a puppy, a new born little potato. One shot. Gone. 
After a whole day of growth and many fights won, it's frustating... I can accept mega packs, and some mix packs too, but this sucks.
love it but the voip servers are better so in the future i would add proximity chat to main servers too.

However they seem to care more about deleting comments from said hacking victims because they said what color skin they "had". Instead of investigating and dealing with the plague of hackers in the game.

I can not recommend this game if you care about your time, it will not be respected.

Only reason this game is "good" is because it has virtually no competition. PoT and BoB are too arcade-y to be considered actual competition. Nothing compares to the isle when it comes the realism the isle brings, yet. Echos of Extinction and Primeval horizon are 2 games that havent been released yet but look very promising. Hopefully at least 1 of them will have devs that care more than dondi. So many things wrong with this game that we have been begging to get fixed for YEARS. Devs just say theyre working on it but it doesnt feel like they are. They also REFUSE to IP ban hackers. They want them to just buy the game again and keep on hacking

Still bad at the game.

10/10 would recommend to a friend

map is massive you can literally go hours without seeing anyone in a server of 115 people 
 theres ai pigs and creatures but they dont spawn everywhere so hotspots of players form in areas of the gigantic map but theres no navigation or meaningful landmarks so you kind of have to just die repeatedly of starvation until you figure it out i love the concept tried to enjoy it for 12 hours but died of boredom the mechanics are actually fun in terms of chat combat ect but the gigantic lifeless map ruins the experience wouldnt recomend it even if it was free

One of the most unique survival games ever made, which makes the dev team's priorities all the more baffling.

The concept is brilliant. The execution is inconsistent to the point of embarrassment. 

Fish spawns are broken. As Deinosuchus or Pterodon you'll starve by every river because nothing spawns for 20 minutes. 

Stamina regen takes literally five minutes of doing nothing. Night vision is so dark the game is unplayable at night despite night being a core mechanic. 

Pteranodon has no wing sounds. Lol. 

Friendly fire kills packmates because the team detection system can't handle more than a handful of players. There's no suicide button (just like real life, sadly) so dying slowly takes forever.

Way too many carnivores. Playing herbivore is a punishment.

None of these are hard fixes. All of them have been reported repeatedly. None are addressed while new content gets announced.

The bones of a masterpiece. Run by people who seemingly don't play their own game.

6/10, would get eaten by my bird-dad again. Buy on release I guess.
Do not buy this game until the anti-cheat is actually functional. Died to more 1-shotting cheaters in the last month than I have in the last 8 years of playing; official admins do not care.

If you're looking for a slow-paced survival, where you never feel really safe, and love to be prudent and patient, this is a must-try. The fact that pretty much anything you encounter will be a player and not an NPC is super engaging. Plus, there are a ton of unofficial servers with different modifiers (rules for dino herds so the game is always playable, growth multipliers fif you wanna reach adulthood faster, etc...)
It's still way indev, the gameplay loop is still simple, optimization is not there yet, and there are gonna be a few bugs and crashes, but it is already super playable, wich wasnt the case a few years back. So thank you devs for going on with this project !

My advice at the time I write all this : play the Evrima version (right click once installed > properties > game versions and betas > select Evrima), and on an unofficial server. There are tons with different rules, usually paired with their own discord server.

Survive, find a mate and make little ones, eat and get eaten and maybe... reach Prime stage ?

This game has so many bugs its awful. I really wanted to play this game but now I can't even get onto a server to play the game. Don't waste your money. Not worth it!
love the game sadly not a lot of things to eat as carnivore but great other wise

This game survives for two reasons. The first is that it has basically no real competition when it comes to playing as realistic dinosaurs. The second is the The Isle community, whose feet the developers should honestly be kissing. Even though these people will happily eat you on sight in-game, they’re also the only reason this game is still alive.

New players should not expect to buy this game and immediately have fun. That’s simply not how The Isle works. Almost everything you actually need to know is taught by the community, not by the game itself.

Need to install the correct version of the game? Learn how to navigate the map? Figure out where other players, food, and water sources are located? Understand how groups work? You’ll end up searching through YouTube videos, Discord servers, forum posts, and community-made guides just to understand the basics.

To properly play this game at all, you are forced to rely on community-made content because almost everything official, including the official servers, is poorly managed and poorly explained.

It genuinely feels like going back to the 90s, when you had to dig through random shady forums just to figure out how a game worked. It’s frustrating, and I honestly do not understand why the developers refuse to address it.

For example, almost the entire playerbase relies on external websites just to use a map of the game world. If nearly everyone already depends on third-party maps, then why not simply add one into the game itself through a button or menu option?

Whenever players don’t know something, they have to ask the community. How to cure diseases, how to unstuck yourself after getting trapped by a bug, what commands exist, how to invite friends into a group, none of this is properly explained in-game. The solution is not complicated. The simplest possible implementation would be to add a basic “Manual” section to the main menu with explanations, controls, commands, and images showing how the systems work, the same way many games used to do back in the 90s.

spawned in, spent an hour running around in the dark attempting to find my friends and attempted to figure out how the game works, no tutorial so that sucked. Broke my leg  twice after falling off a cliff and then got bulldozed by a big dyno.  just to respond  back in the dark to run around to find my friend again... after another hour in the dark i broke my leg again.... need a refund to buy band aids, and Dino nuggets because they can be more fun.

Ran around for 2 plus hours just to kill one dino. My buddy then proceeded to fall down a steep cliff and broke his small pinky toe, where he was out of commission for an hour. They should call this game night simulator, because most of your time in game will be in a pitch black forest where the only thing you can hear are the cries of other gamers wanting a refund for this game. Just go outside and touch grass it will be a lot more fun and worth your time unlike this game. $20 could be spent on better things other than this game. No like seriously, anything!

Nice game concept but there are too many hackers in this game with auto tracking who can follow you through dense forrest for 5 mins 100 meters away or even just see you in situations where they shouldn't. Also there is no in game map, and dodgy terrain can wipe 20 hours worth of progress in just a few seconds. Not to mention groups of mix packers from different species ganging up on solo players because they can. Honestly horrible unless you enjoy suffering outside of your control.

I love this game, diversity of dinosaurs with more still being added. The only things I would mention is that the game still can be ever so slightly buggy at times with server lag and that mix packs are a massive annoyance when you are playing alone or just with one other person as they usually now consist of like 10 mixed dino's like t-rex, trike, allo, etc. Other than that I enjoy the game and think it can be quite peaceful at times and extremely fun with friends!

As a dinosaur lover, i'm obsessed! 
not going to lie there is absolutly no tutorials and nothing to hold your hand, but about 5 youtube walkthroughs later it made enough sence to play the game without too much panic! 
the horror of you might die at every corner from another player is exciting and again once you have a handle of the basics settles after a few hours, then you focus on growing and learning and attempting to make contact with others. 
If your not a survival fan or dinosaur fan i don't recommend it, the joy is to live how a dino would and over all game play is basic and can get tidius. 
But i absolotly love this game and can't wait till i have a free few hours to jump on and play it again! 

(reminded me of ark, but dinosaurs instead of humans and no building or creating)

I have never been so frustrated with a game in my life. I have put hours and hours into grinding this game, only for it to constantly crash and completely wipe out all of my save data. It doesn't just happen once; it happens repeatedly. Every time I get kicked out, my progress is gone forever, and I am forced to restart from absolute zero.

To make matters worse, the developers are completely ghosting the community. I have tried reaching out to support, and they don't respond. They are nowhere to be found and clearly do not care that people are losing their time and money on a fundamentally broken product.

Save your money and your sanity. Do not buy this trash until they actually fix the save bugs and show up to support their players.

Potentially a great game, but completely ruined by dev indifference. There is a persistent bug that randomly wipes your dino after hours of progress. I highly recommend this game, but it has some important shortcomings you need to be aware of...

1) Too few servers that are all (on the US front, at least) maxed on the evenings/weekends requiring you to repeatedly hit connect for 15+ minutes.
2) Half of the dinosaurs are useless and never played; almost all play T-rex, raptor, allosaurus, trike, stego, carno, cera or deinosuchus (alligator).  
3) Some major bugs have been around for a long time and still not fixed.
4) Although not as bad as ARK, there is still quite a bit of hacking used against you in this game.

Having said that, the game IS fun, but make sure you Google getting and understanding "prime" as it totally changes the game for you.  Lastly, this game shines brightest when played with a friend(s).  I am more of a solo player in most games (even MMOs), but I have had the best times ever when rolling with other in The Aisle.

Don't get me wrong, this game is peak when it comes to the living as a dinosaur. But the fact that this game has been out since 2015 and has such a minimal dinosaur selection is wild to me. I mean I'll still play it all the time but jeez.. give us something to work with.

Im not saying its a bad game, its just not my kind of game.

This game is over ten years old now and somehow feels less and less finished with every update.
Mechanics are added in nonsensical orders. Maps are created, then changed in ways that make them actively worse, then abandoned, over and over again. Broken features remain broken for years inbetween updates. Changes are made to things that actually function well for seemingly no reason, making them worse as a result (Ai spawns, Menu UI, the list goes on).
Just to give an example here, servers used to allow you to queue up for them, waiting in line for a spot to open, but for some baffling reason that feature was removed. So instead of just letting in people one at a time in order while you do something else, you're forced to try over and over again, reloading and retrying nonstop. Who thought that was a good idea? How do you make the process of starting the game go from perfectly fine to actively annoying and call it an improvement? :/

For every nice player you meet or cool fight you'll have, you'll come across another player using cheats or exploits. For every fun moment you have, you'll spend hours waiting around doing nothing or dying due to things that aren't your fault, like random glitches or bugs that won't get fixed in the same decade they are introduced in.
The only real objectively good thing about The Isle is how visually stunning it can be on the highest settings, but it's so poorly optimized that you'll probably struggle to even run it on the lowest.
And all of that isn't even mentioning the tons of drama surrounding some of the developers that is somehow so bad that you'll come across it even when you're actively trying to avoid it.

A dino survival game has so much potential for fun, but this just isn't it.

This game is great on pen and paper, is extremely fun and I really hope they fix it but seeing as it's almost 10 years old now I doubt it.

Absolutely no reason my dino's should be dying, yet when I log back on, they are gone and I have to restart 5-6 hours worth of progress.

ENG
I do not recommend The Isle Evrima. The idea behind the game is fantastic, and the atmosphere can genuinely be impressive at times, but almost everything else performs far worse than it should especially for a game that has been in development for so many years.
The biggest issue is the terrible technical state of the game. Optimization is extremely poor. Even on stronger PCs, massive FPS drops, stuttering, and random lag spikes happen constantly for no clear reason. Almost every major update tends to introduce new bugs or break existing mechanics instead of improving the game. It often feels like the developers are experimenting rather than actually polishing the product.
The game is filled with bugs. Dinosaurs get stuck on rocks, fall through the map, or randomly die for no reason. Sometimes hitboxes simply do not work  an enemy bites the air and you still take damage. Other times you land multiple perfect hits and the game does not register them at all. Server desync is extremely common, which turns combat into a frustrating gamble instead of something skill-based and exciting. The most frustrating part is spending hours growing your dinosaur only to lose everything because of a crash or glitch.
What is even more irritating is the developers’ approach to the game’s development. Instead of finishing one feature properly, they constantly start new systems and mechanics while leaving previous ones unfinished. The game is full of mechanics that feel like early test versions, yet they are still pushed into the public build even though they are clearly not ready. Soon after, the developers move on to another new feature, which also ends up abandoned or released in a half-finished state. Because of this, the entire game feels chaotic and directionless  like a never-ending alpha with no solid foundation. Instead of polished systems, players constantly receive “promises of potential” that are never truly completed.
Balance is another major issue. Some dinosaurs are clearly overpowered while others feel completely useless. PvP often comes down to abusing the current meta rather than actual skill. On top of that, many players exploit broken mechanics and bugs to gain unfair advantages.
Another problem is the painfully slow development pace. Evrima was supposed to be a “new beginning,” yet after all these years it still feels like a very unfinished alpha version. Updates take forever to release, roadmaps constantly change, and many promised features either never arrive or release in an extremely limited form. Part of the community defends this by saying “it’s Early Access,” but how long can that excuse realistically be used? At some point, players have the right to expect a stable and polished product.
Communication from the developers does not help either. Criticism often feels ignored or even mocked by parts of the development team and community. Instead of building trust, the developers repeatedly create controversy and divide the playerbase. Because of this, the overall atmosphere surrounding the game feels strange — many people seem to play more out of hope that “it will get better someday” rather than because the game is genuinely good right now.

It is honestly disappointing because the game does have moments of real potential. Night hunts, dinosaur calls echoing through the forest, and first encounters with other players can be genuinely immersive and exciting. The problem is that those good moments are buried under layers of frustration, bugs, terrible optimization, and painfully slow progress. After dozens of hours, I felt more exhausted than satisfied.

PL
Nie polecam The Isle Evrima. Pomysł jest świetny, klimat momentami naprawdę potrafi zrobić wrażenie, ale praktycznie wszystko inne nie działa, niż powinno  szczególnie jak na grę rozwijaną od tylu lat.
Największym problemem jest tragiczny stan techniczny gry. Optymalizacja jest bardzo słaba. Nawet na mocniejszych komputerach zdarzają się ogromne spadki FPS, stuttering i ściny bez wyraźnego powodu. Każda większa aktualizacja zamiast poprawiać stan gry często dodaje nowe błędy albo psuje stare mechaniki. Można odnieść wrażenie, że deweloperzy bardziej eksperymentują niż faktycznie dopracowują produkt.
Bugów jest ogrom. Dinozaury potrafią blokować się o kamienie, wpadać pod mapę albo ginąć z losowych powodów. Czasami hitboxy kompletnie nie działają, przeciwnik gryzie powietrze, a i tak otrzymujesz obrażenia. Innym razem trafiasz idealnie kilka razy i gra tego nie rejestruje. Desynchronizacja na serwerach jest bardzo częsta i przez to walka zamiast być emocjonująca, staje się loterią. Najbardziej frustrujące jest to, że możesz spędzić kilka godzin rozwijając swojego dinozaura, żeby później stracić wszystko przez crash gry albo glitch.
Najbardziej irytujące jest jednak podejście deweloperów do rozwoju gry. Zamiast dopracować jedną rzecz do końca, ciągle zaczynają kolejne systemy i funkcje, zostawiając poprzednie niedokończone. W grze jest masa mechanik, które wyglądają jak wersje testowe, a mimo to trafiają do publicznej wersji, choć ewidentnie nie są gotowe. Po chwili twórcy przerzucają uwagę na coś nowego, co później również zostaje porzucone albo wypuszczone w połowie ukończone. Przez to cała gra sprawia wrażenie chaosu i wiecznej alfy bez konkretnego kierunku. Zamiast solidnych fundamentów dostajemy kolejne „zapowiedzi potencjału”, które nigdy nie są naprawdę kończone.
Balans także pozostawia wiele do życzenia. Niektóre dinozaury są wyraźnie zbyt mocne, inne praktycznie nie mają sensu istnieć. PvP często sprowadza się do wykorzystywania aktualnie najmocniejszej mety zamiast faktycznych umiejętności. Do tego dochodzą gracze exploitujący mechaniki czy wykorzystujący bugi.
Kolejna rzecz to tempo rozwoju gry. Evrima miała być „nowym początkiem”, a mimo to po tylu latach nadal sprawia wrażenie bardzo niedokończonej wersji alfa. Aktualizacje wychodzą bardzo wolno, roadmapy zmieniają się ciągle, a wiele obiecanych funkcji albo nie trafia do gry przez lata, albo pojawia się w mocno okrojonej wersji. Część społeczności broni tego mówiąc, że „to Early Access”, ale ile można używać tego argumentu? W pewnym momencie gracze mają prawo oczekiwać stabilnego i dopracowanego produktu.
Komunikacja deweloperów również nie pomaga. Często odnoszę wrażenie, że krytyka jest ignorowana albo wyśmiewana przez część zespołu i społeczności. Zamiast budować zaufanie, twórcy regularnie wywołują kontrowersje i dzielą graczy. Przez to atmosfera wokół gry jest bardzo dziwna  wiele osób gra bardziej z nadziei, że „kiedyś będzie lepiej”, niż dlatego, że obecnie jest dobrze.

Szkoda, bo momenty potencjału naprawdę tutaj istnieją. Nocne polowania, odgłosy dinozaurów w lesie czy pierwsze spotkania z innymi graczami potrafią być świetne. Problem w tym, że te pojedyncze dobre chwile są zasypane toną frustracji, bugów, słabej optymalizacji i powolnego rozwoju. Po kilkudziesięciu godzinach bardziej czułem zmęczenie niż satysfakcję.

Can we do something about the character save file just being just occasionally deleted randomly upon log in. (official servers,  yes i safe logged, its happened multiple times. ) I'm really tired of putting so much time into a character only for my hours of sitting in bushes to just poof due to a random bug the next time I try to boot the game. Like what's even the point of the entomb system when you can't trust the systems to even remember your character exists.

While I love this game and have spent over 400 hours playing it in the last month, the developers and their public relations are terrible. The company's name, "Afterthought LLC" is very fitting. They do not have any forward thinking or planning to the things they do. Everything is an "afterthought". They do not care if they deleted your dino that you spent 20+ hours to grow. They could care less. Their solution: Play on unofficial servers. This statement shows their incompetence.

The Isle is what happens when someone takes every modern gaming convenience, throws it into a volcano, and replaces it with pure anxiety. You'll spend hours growing your dinosaur, carefully avoiding predators, managing hunger, and questioning every noise in the bushes. Then some oversized murder lizard appears from nowhere and sends your entire evening back to the respawn screen.

And somehow, you'll queue up and do it all again.

When The Isle is firing on all cylinders, there's nothing quite like it. The atmosphere is unmatched. Every drink from a river feels risky, every distant roar means something, and every player encounter can become a friendship, a betrayal, or a very short obituary.

The downside? Development moves slower than a starving stegosaurus with a broken leg. Bugs still show up, balance changes can be questionable, and sometimes you'll wonder if the game's biggest predator is actually the update schedule.

Yet despite all of that, The Isle remains the closest thing to actually being a dinosaur. It's brutal, unforgiving, occasionally infuriating, and capable of creating stories you'll remember weeks later.

Would I recommend it? Absolutely.

Would I recommend getting emotionally attached to your dinosaur? Not unless you enjoy grief.

Would love to give the game a good review, but after spending hours growing my croc only to have the game delete the deino (I safe logged) hell yeah ggs.


**Edit** decided to do a Allo because they dont take as long. Logged in today, It was wiped. Yeah, games uninstalled.
